name: Satyanarayan Puja
deity: Lord Satyanarayan (Vishnu as the True One)
duration_note: Typically performed in an afternoon session of 1.5–2 hours. Not a fasting ritual — it is a puja + katha (storytelling) event.

recurrence.repeat_note: Most commonly performed on Purnima (full moon) or on special occasions: housewarming, promotions, anniversaries, childbirth, business inaugurations. Can also be done on any auspicious day.

sankalpa_text_template: ममात्मनः श्रेयःसिद्ध्यर्थं सकलपापक्षयपूर्वकं धर्मार्थकाममोक्षचतुर्विधफलप्राप्त्यर्थं श्रीसत्यनारायणदेवप्रसादसिद्ध्यर्थं च सत्यनारायणव्रतपूजां करिष्ये ।
sankalpa_guide: Sit facing east. Hold water and a flower. Say your name, gotra, and the occasion (Purnima / Griha Pravesh / etc.), then recite the sankalpa. Release the water.

preparation: Choose the occasion and invite family/friends — Satyanarayan Puja is a communal event, not a solitary ritual
Prepare the prasad (Sheera): Semolina (sooji) fried in ghee, then add milk, sugar, banana, and dried fruits. Make enough for all attendees.
Set up the altar: yellow cloth, Vishnu's image/idol, a Kalash (pot of water with mango leaves and coconut on top)
Prepare puja items: yellow flowers, Tulsi leaves, incense, ghee lamp, sandalwood paste
Print or have the Satyanarayan Katha available — you will read all 5 chapters during the puja
Fast is optional but preferred on the day until the puja is complete

materials.name: Sooji (semolina)
materials.purpose: Main ingredient for the Sheera prasad — Satyanarayan's special offering
materials.substitute: No substitute for Sheera — it is the defining element of this puja

materials.name: Ghee (clarified butter)
materials.purpose: For frying the sooji and for the lamp
materials.substitute: Cannot be substituted in Sheera — must use ghee

materials.name: Banana
materials.purpose: Goes into the Sheera prasad — ripens and adds sweetness

materials.name: Tulsi leaves
materials.purpose: Essential for Vishnu puja — placed on the Kalash and offered with each mantra

materials.name: Yellow flowers (marigold)
materials.purpose: Vishnu's flowers are yellow — this puja is vibrant and festive

materials.name: Kalash (copper or silver pot)
materials.purpose: Represents Lord Vishnu — decorated with mango leaves and topped with coconut

materials.name: Pancha-amrit (5 nectars)
materials.quantity: Milk, curd, honey, ghee, sugar — small quantities

materials.name: Panchaphala (5 fruits)
materials.purpose: Offering to Vishnu — fruits represent the fullness of creation

materials.name: Sat-pan-diya (7 wicks lamp)
materials.purpose: For the aarti — 7-wick lamp is traditional for Satyanarayan Puja

steps.title: Purification and Setup
steps.action: Bathe. Set up the altar with yellow cloth, Vishnu's image/idol, Kalash, and flowers. Place the Sheera (prepared prasad) covered with a cloth — it will be offered later.
steps.note: The Sheera should be prepared before guests arrive so it's ready to offer. Keep it warm.

steps.title: Ganesha Puja (Always First)
steps.action: Begin any puja with Ganesha. Light the lamp. Offer durva grass and modak to Ganesha's image or the sankalp. Chant 'Om Gam Ganapataye Namah' 3 times.
steps.note: Never begin any puja without Ganesha's permission. He removes the obstacles that would interrupt the puja.

steps.title: Sankalpa
steps.action: Priest/elder leads the sankalpa. All participants touch water together (or each holds a flower). Sankalpa is recited: name, occasion, intent.
steps.note: When doing this with family, all members ideally participate in the sankalpa — even children.

steps.title: Kalash Puja
steps.action: Invoke all gods into the Kalash — place Tulsi leaves into the water. Offer flowers to the Kalash. Touch the coconut on top with sandalwood paste and kumkum. The Kalash is now Lord Vishnu's temporary abode.

steps.title: Shodashopachara Puja — 16-Step Vishnu Worship
steps.action: Offer each of the 16 items (Dhyana, Avahana, Asana... through Namaskara) to Lord Vishnu's image or the Kalash. Chant each step's mantra while making the offering.
steps.note: If you don't know all 16 steps, even offering flowers, incense, and food with the mantra 'Om Namo Narayanaya' for each is valid.

steps.title: Satyanarayan Katha — Reading the 5 Stories
steps.action: Sit comfortably. Read all 5 chapters of the Satyanarayan Katha aloud. All participants listen with attention — the stories carry the actual teaching of the puja. Tulsi leaves should be in hand while listening.
steps.note: The katha is not optional. The puja's merit comes from hearing the stories with attention. If someone leaves the room, they come back with full attention — the story must be heard completely.

steps.title: Naivedya — Offering the Sheera
steps.action: Uncover the prepared Sheera. Place it before Vishnu's image. Offer it with 'Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya' and the Satyanarayan Prasad Mantra. Light fresh incense at this moment.
steps.note: The Sheera must be offered to the Lord BEFORE anyone eats. This is the defining moment of the puja.

steps.title: Aarti — Jay Lakshmi Ramana
steps.action: Wave the ghee lamp in clockwise circles before the deity while all participants sing the Satyanarayan Aarti. Ring the bell throughout. After aarti, everyone prostrates.

steps.title: Prasad Distribution
steps.action: Distribute the Sheera to all participants. Accept with both hands, eat with devotion. This is not just food — it is the Lord's direct blessing.
steps.note: The fourth chapter of the Satyanarayan Katha specifically warns against disrespecting the prasad. Accept it, eat at least a small spoonful.

steps.title: Kshama Prarthana
steps.action: Elder or priest recites the Kshama Prarthana for any errors in the puja. All bow together.

mantras_explained.why_this_mantra: The Satyanarayan Sankalpa mentions all four Purusharthas: Dharma, Artha, Kama, Moksha. Most people do this puja for Artha (prosperity). But the mantra says: I seek all four. This is Vishnu's teaching — do not be ashamed of material wants, but do not stop there either. Satyanarayan gives abundance and guides you toward liberation at the same time.
mantras_explained.what_to_visualize: Lord Vishnu in Vaikuntha, four-armed, gold-complexioned, Lakshmi seated beside him. He is smiling at you the way a wealthy, loving uncle smiles at a nephew who asks for help — fully happy to give, fully capable of giving.

mantras_explained.why_this_mantra: The prasad mantra transforms a bowl of semolina into divine food. Not metaphorically — the act of offering, combined with the story of the Lord receiving offerings with joy, changes how the food is received by those who eat it. The katha's warning: those who received this prasad but disrespected it lost everything. Those who accepted it with humility prospered.
mantras_explained.how_to_pronounce: Om Na-mo Bha-ga-va-te Vaa-su-de-vaa-ya | Nai-ved-yam Sa-mar-pa-yaa-mi | Sa-tya-naa-raa-ya-naa-ya Na-mah
mantras_explained.what_to_visualize: See the bowl of Sheera glowing golden as you offer it. The Lord reaches forward and accepts your offering — the same hands that hold the universe accept your bowl of sweet pudding. That is Bhakti: God receiving what you give, however small.

alarms.label: Prepare Satyanarayan Sheera
alarms.notification_message: Prepare Satyanarayan Sheera and set up the puja altar — guests arrive later

alarms.label: Satyanarayan Puja — Begin
alarms.notification_message: Time to begin Satyanarayan Puja — gather family and guests

materials_disposal.disposal_method: Pour on a plant or in the garden — or sprinkle around the house for purification
materials_disposal.note: This water is now sanctified — it is called 'Charanamrit'

materials_disposal.disposal_method: Break and distribute to all present as prasad
Place at the base of the Tulsi plant or in the garden
Immerse in water or bury in garden
Distribute to all family and neighbors. No Sheera should be wasted.
materials_disposal.note: Offering Sheera to neighbors is itself a meritorious act (Annadaan)

after_completion.prasad_distribution: Distribute Sheera to all present. Then send portions to immediate neighbors and elderly relatives who could not attend. Every person who receives the Sheera participates in the puja's merit.
next_steps: Mark this Satyanarayan Puja as completed
If performing monthly (Purnima): next puja is next Purnima — app will remind 3 days before
Share the Satyanarayan Katha stories with children — the stories are simple enough for children to understand and remember

udyapan.description: Satyanarayan Puja is self-contained and does not require Udyapan after a fixed count. However, if you vowed to perform it for a specific occasion (house completion, business success), then once the occasion is fulfilled, perform one more puja as a thank-you.
